2603.22910.title: EchoKV: Efficient KV Cache Compression via Similarity-Based Reconstruction
2603.22910.paper_summary: The increasing memory demand of the Key-Value (KV) cache poses a significant bottleneck for Large Language Models (LLMs) in long-context applications. Existing low-rank compression methods often rely on irreversible parameter transformations, sacrificing the flexibility to switch back to full-precision inference when memory is abundant. In this paper, we propose EchoKV, a flexible KV cache compression scheme that enables on-demand transitions between standard and compressed inference. Unlike traditional compression-decompression paradigms, EchoKV utilizes a lightweight network to reconstruct the residual KV components from a partial subset, leveraging intrinsic inter-layer and intra-layer similarities among attention heads. We further introduce a two-stage fine-tuning strategy that allows for rapid, low-cost training (e.g., ~1 A100 GPU-hour for a 7B model). Experimental results on LongBench and RULER demonstrate that EchoKV consistently outperforms existing methods across various compression ratios while maintaining high throughput for short-context scenarios.
2603.22910.paper_summary_zh: 隨著鍵值（KV）快取的記憶體需求不斷增加，這對於長上下文應用中的大型語言模型（LLMs）造成了顯著的瓶頸。現有的低秩壓縮方法通常依賴於不可逆的參數轉換，犧牲了在記憶體充足時切換回全精度推理的靈活性。在本文中，我們提出了EchoKV，一種靈活的KV快取壓縮方案，能夠實現標準推理和壓縮推理之間的按需轉換。與傳統的壓縮-解壓縮範式不同，EchoKV利用輕量級網路從部分子集重建殘餘的KV組件，利用注意力頭之間和層內的內在相似性。我們進一步引入了一種兩階段的微調策略，允許快速、低成本的訓練（例如，對於一個7B模型約需1個A100 GPU小時）。在LongBench和RULER上的實驗結果表明，EchoKV在各種壓縮比下始終優於現有方法，同時在短上下文場景中保持高吞吐量。
2603.22910.author: Yixuan Wang et.al.
2603.22910.authors: Yixuan Wang, Shiyu Ji, Yijun Liu, Qingfu Zhu, Wanxiang Che
